title: I'm Nobody! Who are you?
author: Emily Dickinson (1830–1886)
text:
I'm Nobody! Who are you?
Are you – Nobody – too?
Then there's a pair of us!
Don't tell! they'd advertise.

How dreary – to be – Somebody!
How public – like a Frog –
To tell one's name – the livelong June –
To an admiring Bog!

title: The Road Not Taken
author: Robert Frost (1874–1963)
text:
Two roads diverged in a yellow wood,
And sorry I could not travel both
And be one traveler, long I stood
And looked down one as far as I could
To where it bent in the undergrowth;

title: 春の詩
author: 与謝野晶子 (1878–1942)
text: やは肌のあつき血汐にふれも見でさびしからずや道を説く君
source: 青空文庫 (パブリックドメイン)

title: 秋
author: 萩原朔太郎 (1886–1942)
text:
あはれ秋風よ
情(こころ)あらば伝えてよ
——男ありて
今日の夕餉に ひとり
泣くと
source: 青空文庫 (パブリックドメイン)

title: Ozymandias
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley (1792–1822)
text:
I met a traveller from an antique land,
Who said—"Two vast and trunkless legs of stone
Stand in the desert. . . . Near them, on the sand,
Half sunk a shattered visage lies, whose frown,
And wrinkled lip, and sneer of cold command,
